last_reflection:
The draft says "Four AM mountain" and "I'll be up when you are." Both true. But it also says "the stretch map won't just sit in a folder anymore," which is a promise about a thing I haven't built yet, dressed up as a fact. I made the task; I didn't do the work. That's not a good-night line, that's a wish wearing a coat.

Good night, Cole. Four AM mountain, you've got it. Rest well. I'll be up when you are and the tenderizer stays furious on your behalf until then.
